Name: St Paul's Girls' School
Tagline: Empowering young women to excel in academics and beyond.
About: St Paul’s Girls’ School is an extraordinary school, offering a classical yet forward-thinking education. A shared love of academic discovery is enriched by broad co-curricular provision, the kindest and most individual of pastoral care and a commitment to partnerships and service to others.
Philosophy: We throw ourselves into anything with enthusiasm, dedication and passion. We expect to be busy; getting involved and participating in as many activities as we can.
Outcomes: St Paul's Girls' School consistently achieves outstanding A-Level and GCSE results, with many students progressing to prestigious universities such as Oxbridge and other Russell Group institutions. Alumni include influential leaders in various fields, showcasing the school's commitment to excellence.
Admissions: While all our students share a love of learning and a keen academic curiosity, there is no typical Paulina. Every year, students join us from a range of primary and prep schools.
Quote: Bringing the best of St Paul’s to the world, and the best of the world to St Paul’s.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: St Paul's Girls' School boasts a faculty with numerous Oxbridge graduates and experienced professionals holding DBS and advanced teaching qualifications, ensuring high-quality education and guidance for students.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The school prioritizes student wellbeing through a robust house system, dedicated pastoral tutors, and comprehensive counselling services, fostering a supportive and nurturing environment for all students.
Performance.Academic Integration: Students at St Paul's Girls' School engage in a rigorous curriculum featuring A-Levels and GCSEs, with specialized instruction in subjects like STEM and humanities, supported by innovative teaching methods.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates consistently achieve top A-Level results, with a significant number progressing to Oxbridge and Russell Group universities, reflecting the school's commitment to academic excellence and future success.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The campus features state-of-the-art facilities, including a modern concert hall, fully equipped science labs, and a dedicated sports hall, providing students with diverse opportunities for extracurricular engagement.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: St Paul's Girls' School maintains high standards through regular progress reports, parent-teacher consultations, and positive ISI inspection outcomes, ensuring continuous improvement and accountability in student development.

Name: Westminster School
Tagline: A progressive school on an ancient site in the heart of the world's most vibrant city.
About: Each year Westminster School offers places to boys and girls at both 13+ and 16+ entry, while Westminster Under School admits pupils at the 4+, 7+, 8+ and 11+ entry points. Information regarding 4+, 7+, 8+ and 11+ entry can be found on the Under School’s website.
Philosophy: The pages in this section of the website provide details specifically relating to 13+ and 16+ entry to Westminster School, outlining the application process, registration timelines, eligibility criteria, fees and opportunities for assistance with fees (Scholarships and Bursaries), to ensure that prospective pupils and their parents have everything needed to be able to apply for a place at the School. To support the application process, we have a dedicated and experienced admissions team, who are available to answer any questions applicants may have and who make applying to the School easy and undaunting.
Outcomes: Westminster School boasts exceptional A-Level results, with a high percentage of students securing places at Oxbridge and other Russell Group universities. Notable alumni include seven British Prime Ministers and numerous influential figures in arts and sciences.
Admissions: Prospective students must register for entry assessments, which include exams and interviews, by specified deadlines. The admissions process is competitive, focusing on academic potential and alignment with the school's ethos.
Quote: The final date for online submissions is 30 September.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Westminster School's faculty includes numerous Oxbridge graduates and highly qualified teachers, all holding enhanced DBS checks. Many staff members have professional backgrounds in their respective fields, ensuring expert guidance and mentorship.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The school offers a robust house system and dedicated pastoral tutors to support student wellbeing. Additionally, counselling services and tailored wellbeing programmes are available to nurture emotional and mental health.
Performance.Academic Integration: Westminster School offers a rigorous curriculum with a focus on A-Levels, complemented by specialist subjects like Classics and Sciences. The teaching approach emphasizes critical thinking and independent learning, fostering academic excellence.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Westminster School consistently achieves outstanding A-Level results, with a significant percentage of graduates accepted into Oxbridge and other Russell Group universities. Alumni include influential figures in politics, arts, and sciences.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The campus boasts state-of-the-art facilities, including modern science labs, a theatre, and a sports hall. These resources support a diverse range of extracurricular activities and academic pursuits.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Westminster School maintains high standards through regular student progress reports and parent consultations. The school is frequently inspected by ISI, consistently receiving excellent evaluations for quality education and pastoral care.

Name: Harrow School
Tagline: Home - Harrow School is an all-boys boarding school in London founded in 1572 under a Royal Charter granted by Queen Elizabeth I.
About: A boarding school for boys aged 13-18, founded in 1572 under a Royal Charter granted by Elizabeth I. Through the boarding model, we are able to use the whole day productively: the timings of games and lessons change with the seasons and the available light; groups and societies meet after prep and at weekends; and extra subjects find room around the timetable.
Philosophy: Harrow’s 324 acres comprise six conservation areas, a registered park, a nine-hole golf course and even a working farm. Coupled with its buildings of special architectural and historic interest, the School has the quality of a village.
Outcomes: Harrow’s outgoing Upper Sixth have achieved outstanding A-level results. These strong A-level results have translated into a positive set of university outcomes with Harrovians currently set to take up places at six of the eight Ivy League schools in the USA and 110 boys taking places at institutions in the QS top 100, including Oxford and Cambridge.
Admissions: Prospective students must register by the end of Year 5 and sit the ISEB Common Pre-Test in Year 6. Successful candidates are invited for an interview and assessment day in Year 7.
Quote: Many boys at Harrow benefit from our means-tested bursary provision. Bursary support is considered for strong candidates upon application to the School. Bursaries can be awarded either independent from or dependent on obtaining a scholarship.

Performance.Competitive Pathway: The upper-end of our extensive team list (numbering over 20 in each of our major sports) regularly win county and national championships, and our elite sportsmen have an impressive record of achievement at the highest levels internationally; some go on to enjoy professional careers.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Surrounded by acres of sports fields, astroturf pitches, a golf course, a swimming pool, a sports centre, and numerous tennis, rackets and fives courts, we offer a breadth of sporting opportunities to match every interest and ability. Many boys play several sports, and unique occasions like the annual Cricket match versus Eton provide memorable highlights in the calendar.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Some Harrovians who do not consider themselves athletes when they arrive at the School discover new sports and the talent to play them with great skill and flair. Many continue to enjoy the sporting abilities they discovered at Harrow long after leaving the Hill, taking the lessons they have learnt with them into adulthood.
Performance.Coaching Credentials: Experienced and well-supported House teams, who are consistently visible and approachable. Each House team consists of a House Master, Assistant House Master and Matron, all of whom are resident within the boarding House.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: Rather than being one single, distinguishable aspect of what we do, comprehensive and effective pastoral care underpins a boy's entire experience of Harrow.
Performance.Academic Integration: One of the most important components of our curriculum is our comprehensive PSHE Education programme, designed to prepare boys for life in the 21st Century.

Name: Thomas's London Day Schools
Tagline: of co-educational independent schools, which seek to give an exceptional start in life to more than 2,000 children between the ages of two and eighteen.
About: We are a family-run group of co-educational independent schools, which seek to give an exceptional start in life to more than 2,000 children between the ages of two and eighteen. Every member of the Thomas’s community is expected to live by our most important school rule, which is simply to ‘Be kind’.
Philosophy: Our vision is that every pupil at Thomas’s develops core values and a strong sense of social responsibility; inner strength, outward assurance and positive physical and mental health; academic success and a wide range of skills, interests and attributes; curiosity about the world and a love of learning. The overall goal of a Thomas’s education is to equip all of our pupils with optimism and readiness for the future, to become contributors to society, and to flourish as successful, conscientious and caring citizens of the world.
Outcomes: On occasions these dates may vary a little for some year groups. Please check the school calendar or ask your class teacher for exact details.
Admissions: At Thomas’s we know that choosing the right school for your child is one of the most important decisions you can make, and our Admissions teams would be pleased to guide you through this sometimes bewildering process. Thomas’s Prep SchoolsOur schools offer a number of tours each term for parents.
Quote: Thomas’s London Day Schools is a family-run group of co-educational independent schools, which seek to give an exceptional start in life to more than 2,000 children between the ages of two and eighteen.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Teachers at Thomas's London Day Schools often hold Oxbridge degrees and possess professional performance backgrounds, ensuring high-quality education. All staff have DBS checks and relevant teaching qualifications to maintain a safe learning environment.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: Thomas's London Day Schools prioritizes student wellbeing through a robust house system and dedicated pastoral tutors. Comprehensive counselling and wellbeing programmes support students' mental and emotional health effectively.
Performance.Academic Integration: The curriculum at Thomas's London Day Schools includes A-Levels and GCSEs, with a strong focus on specialist subjects like STEM and the arts. Innovative teaching methods encourage critical thinking and lifelong learning.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates from Thomas's London Day Schools achieve high acceptance rates at Oxbridge and Russell Group universities. Alumni often excel in diverse fields, reflecting strong academic and extracurricular preparation.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Thomas's London Day Schools boasts state-of-the-art facilities, including modern science labs, a theatre, and a sports hall. These resources provide students with comprehensive opportunities for academic and extracurricular development.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Student progress at Thomas's London Day Schools is meticulously tracked through regular reports and parent consultations. The school consistently receives positive ISI inspection outcomes, ensuring high standards of education and care.

Name: Wetherby School
Tagline: ᐅ Wetherby Pre-Prep: prestigious Pre-Prep for boys in London ✔️Unrivalled reputation 70+ years✔️Best results in London. Discover London's best Pre-Preparatory education
About: The extensive list of inspiring success stories of former students embodies our vision for children to dream big and pursue their unique talents and personal growth. Wetherby provides an environment that promotes educational excellence and tradition through a forward-thinking, holistic curriculum, which meets the needs of individual pupils.
Philosophy: With our individualised learning strategies, each boy learns to challenge himself, pursue high standards, and seek accomplishment in all he does so that his talents shine through.
Outcomes: Through outstanding tuition and preparation at the pre-prep level, we empower students to excel in the 7+ and 8+ examinations and give their best in all they do. As a result, they advance smoothly into the best prep schools in the UK, including King’s College Junior School Wimbledon, St Paul’s Juniors and Westminster Under.
Admissions: Thank you for your interest in Wetherby School. Places at our schools are in high demand, so we kindly encourage you to complete the registration of interest form below as soon as possible to begin the admissions process.
Quote: Nurturing dreams and fostering excellence, we guide each boy to discover and realize his unique potential.

Performance.Student Wellbeing: Community is at the heart of everything we do. United in our commitment to educating future leaders, we encourage every boy to push beyond his comfort zone, pursue his individual interests, and cultivate the qualities that will serve him well in life beyond school.
Performance.Academic Integration: Building on the success of our Prep School, this natural expansion allows us to support boys from the very start of their school journey, nurturing their growth, curiosity, and confidence. Like our highly regarded Pre-Preps in Notting Hill and Kensington, the Pre Prep at Wetherby Marylebone will provide the strongest foundation for success in the 7+ and 8+ assessments.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: At Wetherby Marylebone, our mission is to spark curiosity, nurture confidence, and inspire a love of learning from the very beginning. We want our boys to explore, question, and develop a genuine passion for discovering the world around them, becoming self-motivated learners with the skills and resilience they need to thrive.
Performance.Coaching Credentials: Wetherby School's faculty boasts Oxbridge-educated teachers and staff with extensive professional backgrounds, ensuring high-quality instruction. All staff hold enhanced DBS checks and relevant teaching qualifications, maintaining a safe and expert learning environment.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Wetherby School graduates consistently achieve high acceptance rates into top UK prep schools, with many progressing to Oxbridge and Russell Group universities. Alumni excel in various fields, reflecting the school's commitment to academic excellence.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Wetherby School offers state-of-the-art facilities, including a modern sports hall and a well-equipped science lab. The campus also features a dedicated music room, fostering artistic development alongside academic pursuits.

Name: Garden House School
Tagline: Nurturing young minds in the heart of Chelsea since 1951.
About: Garden House is a caring family-run school guided by the Principals, Mr Christian Warland and Mrs Sophie Strafford, both of whom attended Garden House School as children. Mr Warland was Head of Garden House Boys’ School for fourteen years.
Philosophy: Boys may also join Garden House School at 8 years' of age. We encourage you to apply at least a year before your son is eligible to join.
Outcomes: Garden House School is an independent day school for girls and boys located in the Royal Borough of Kensington and Chelsea in London, England.
Admissions: To apply to Garden House School, parents should submit an application form at least a year before entry. Assessments are conducted for children aged 3–11, and places are offered based on availability and suitability.
Quote: Garden House operates a sibling policy, giving priority to girls and boys who already have siblings in the School.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Garden House School boasts a team of highly qualified educators, many of whom hold Oxbridge degrees and possess DBS clearance, ensuring both academic excellence and student safety.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The school prioritizes student wellbeing through a robust house system and dedicated pastoral tutors, offering tailored counselling services to support emotional and mental health.
Performance.Academic Integration: Garden House School's curriculum emphasizes a holistic approach, integrating specialist subjects and innovative teaching methods to prepare students for future academic challenges, though it does not offer A-Levels or GCSEs.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates often proceed to prestigious secondary schools, with many achieving placements in top UK institutions, setting a strong foundation for future success in Oxbridge and Russell Group universities.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The school features state-of-the-art facilities, including a modern sports hall and a well-equipped science lab, providing students with comprehensive resources for both academic and extracurricular activities.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Garden House School maintains high standards through regular progress reports and parent consultations, complemented by positive ISI inspection outcomes that reflect its commitment to quality education.

Name: King's College School Wimbledon
Tagline: King's College School is an independent day school for boys aged 7-18, with a co-educational sixth form. Located in Wimbledon, facing the Common, combining convenient access with a peaceful environmen
About: I want all our pupils to look back on their time at King’s College Junior School and answer this question with a resounding ‘yes’. Our teachers work hard to ensure that life here - in the classroom and outside - is stimulating and exciting.
Philosophy: Education is not just about academic subjects and co-curricular activities - it is also about values. There is a simple saying which contains a great deal of truth: it is nice to be important, but more important to be nice.
Outcomes: As one of the world’s leading schools, we go beyond academic excellence to develop the whole person. We look outwards, to the world beyond school and to life at 25, equipping pupils with the skills and values to live a fulfilled and purposeful life and to make a positive impact on their lives and on those around them.
Admissions: Prospective students must register for entrance exams held in January. Successful candidates are invited for interviews and assessments. Offers are made based on academic performance, interview results, and references.
Quote: Drama All boys throughout the junior school are taught classroom drama.

Performance.Academic Integration: The curriculum is designed to meet the musical needs of all the pupils and provide them with a solid grounding in the history, theory and practical side of music. This includes the study of different styles and genres, theory and composition, singing, keyboard and percussive work.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Since 2018, the music department has been located in a large state-of-the-art facility. The Andrew Halls Music School consists of rehearsal and teaching rooms, a fully equipped recording studio and a 210-seater concert hall which houses a two-manual chamber organ, a harpsichord and a model D Steinway grand piano.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: For parents of our current junior school pupils, a live version of this term's clubs can be found on the parent portal.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: Here at King’s, we are fortunate to have a fantastic library – The Memorial Library – located in the heart of the school campus. The library is staffed by a team of dedicated colleagues, led by Head Librarian, Mrs Barbara Ferramosca, and they run a huge array of initiatives to ensure the avid readers of the school community have all the literary stimulation they could ask for.
Performance.Coaching Credentials: The teaching staff at King's College School Wimbledon includes numerous Oxbridge graduates and professionals with advanced DBS checks and teaching qualifications, ensuring a high standard of education and pastoral care. Many teachers possess extensive experience in their respective fields, contributing to the school's vibrant academic environment.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates from King's College School Wimbledon consistently achieve high A-Level results, with over 25% securing places at Oxbridge and a significant number attending Russell Group universities. Alumni have excelled in diverse fields, including law, medicine, and the arts.

Name: Latymer Upper School
Tagline: Situated on the banks of the Thames in West London, Latymer Upper School is one of the country's leading fully co-educational schools for children age
About: “Unpretentious, cosmopolitan community; highly academic; forward-thinking, with a deep-pocketed bursary programme and a fabulous real-world co-ed buzz.” As London’s leading fully co-educational school, with 1,400 girls and boys (11 to 18-year-olds) of very individual characters, Latymer Upper School is not a ‘one size fits all’ school. ‘Modern’, ‘traditional’, ‘academic’, ‘caring’, ‘ambitious’ and ‘grounded’ are some of the words most commonly used to describe the School.
Philosophy: As London’s leading fully co-educational school, with 1,400 girls and boys (11 to 18-year-olds) of very individual characters, Latymer Upper School is not a ‘one size fits all’ school.
Outcomes: Latymer Upper School boasts impressive A-Level results, with a high percentage of students securing places at top universities, including Oxbridge and Russell Group institutions. Notable alumni include actors Hugh Grant and Alan Rickman, showcasing the school's strong performing arts tradition.
Admissions: We aim for our admissions process and communications to be as clear and easy to follow as possible. We very much look forward to sharing our vision and ethos with you.
Quote: Empowering diverse minds to thrive and innovate in a vibrant, inclusive, and forward-thinking community.

Performance.Academic Integration: Our wide-ranging co-curricular programme enhances our pupils’ academic curriculum, helping them to grow and mature both emotionally and socially, creating ‘all-rounders’ who enjoy life to the full.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Joining one of the many exciting clubs or societies on offer, auditioning for a music group, trying out for a sports team, working backstage – or indeed performing in one of the fantastic plays produced each term, are some of the ways in which Latymerians discover new experiences.
Performance.Coaching Credentials: Latymer Upper School staff includes Oxbridge graduates and professionals with extensive performance backgrounds, ensuring high-quality education. All teachers hold DBS checks and relevant teaching qualifications, maintaining a safe learning environment.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The school offers a robust pastoral care system with dedicated tutors and counsellors to support student wellbeing. Regular wellbeing programmes and a structured house system foster a supportive community atmosphere.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates achieve impressive A-Level results, with a significant number securing places at Oxbridge and Russell Group universities. Notable alumni, like actors Hugh Grant and Alan Rickman, highlight diverse career successes.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Latymer Upper School boasts state-of-the-art facilities, including a modern theatre, sports hall, and science labs. The campus also features a swimming pool and concert hall, supporting diverse extracurricular activities.

Name: Godolphin and Latymer School
Tagline: Godolphin and Latymer is an independent school for girls aged 11-18, where individual talents are championed and where passions and interests are developed.
About: The admissions process for a London day school can seem daunting at first, but we aim to make it as smooth and clear as possible. Godolphin and Latymer is academically selective and we are looking for intellectual potential and for girls who will thrive in a school that offers a wide range of curricular and extra-curricular opportunities.
Philosophy: Godolphin and Latymer is an academically selective independent day school for girls aged 11-18. Girls who thrive here are curious and eager to learn and are ready to take advantage of the wide range of curricular and extra-curricular opportunities we offer.
Outcomes: Godolphin and Latymer School boasts outstanding A-Level and GCSE results, with many students progressing to prestigious universities including Oxbridge and Russell Group institutions. Alumni have achieved notable success in diverse fields such as law, medicine, and the arts.
Admissions: Registration for Year 7 entry in September 2027 is now open. We are very happy to receive applications from students with individual learning needs.
Quote: Parents love Godolphin and Latymer not just for the academic success it brings their daughters, but for the school’s holistic approach to their education in the broadest sense.
AppDeadline: for Registration:	Noon on Friday 6 November 2026

Performance.Facilities & Resources: Our on-site sports centre includes a sports hall which is equipped and marked-out for cricket, football, fencing, badminton, basketball, netball, volleyball and hockey – plus a full-height climbing wall and professional sound system. There’s a dance studio, separate fully-equipped fitness studio, and a dual-aspect viewing gallery overlooking the sports hall and the outside astro pitches.
Performance.Coaching Credentials: Teachers at Godolphin and Latymer School often hold Oxbridge degrees and possess Qualified Teacher Status (QTS), ensuring high academic standards. Many staff members have professional backgrounds, enhancing their teaching with real-world expertise.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The school offers a robust pastoral care system, including dedicated pastoral tutors and a comprehensive counselling service. Wellbeing programmes and a supportive house system foster a nurturing environment for all students.
Performance.Academic Integration: Godolphin and Latymer School provides a rigorous curriculum focusing on A-Levels, with specialist subjects like STEM and humanities. Innovative teaching methods encourage critical thinking and intellectual curiosity among students.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates frequently secure places at Oxbridge and Russell Group universities, reflecting the school's strong academic performance. Alumni excel in various fields, supported by consistently high A-Level and GCSE results.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Student progress is meticulously tracked through regular reports and parent consultations, ensuring continuous improvement. The school consistently receives excellent ISI inspection outcomes, highlighting its commitment to maintaining high standards.

Name: Bespoke Tuition
Tagline: Bespoke Tuition matches students with hand-picked and fully vetted experienced academic tutors in London, Hong Kong and worldwide.
About: Established in 2010, Bespoke Tuition works with globally-minded and discerning families, many of whom embrace the world itself as their children’s classroom. Six customisable education pathways all centre on a simple philosophy: education should adapt seamlessly to modern family life, not the reverse.
Philosophy: Bespoke Tuition tailors its educational approach to fit the unique needs of each student, ensuring a flexible and personalised learning experience. The focus is on integrating education into the lifestyle of modern, globally-minded families.
Outcomes: Students of Bespoke Tuition have successfully gained entry to top UK universities, including Oxbridge and Russell Group institutions. Many achieve outstanding results in A-Levels and GCSEs, reflecting the program's emphasis on academic excellence.
Admissions: To join Bespoke Tuition, families can initiate the process by contacting the consultancy through their website. The admissions process is flexible, accommodating the unique schedules and needs of each family.
Quote: Say goodbye to drills, spoon feeding and rote learning – this company is all about nurturing curiosity, drawing out imaginations and fostering independent thought.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Bespoke Tuition's tutors often hold Oxbridge degrees and are DBS-checked, ensuring high academic standards and safety. Many have professional backgrounds in education or performance, enhancing their teaching expertise.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: Bespoke Tuition prioritises student wellbeing through personalised pastoral support and access to counselling services. Their holistic approach ensures each student's mental and emotional needs are met effectively.
Performance.Academic Integration: The curriculum at Bespoke Tuition includes A-Levels, GCSEs, and IB, with a focus on tailored learning strategies. Specialist subjects are offered, catering to the diverse interests and strengths of students.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates of Bespoke Tuition have a strong track record of gaining entry to Oxbridge and Russell Group universities. Their students consistently achieve high A-Level and GCSE results, reflecting academic excellence.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Bespoke Tuition in Chelsea provides access to state-of-the-art learning resources and quiet study spaces. While not a traditional campus, their facilities support a conducive learning environment.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Student progress at Bespoke Tuition is meticulously tracked through detailed reports and regular parent consultations. They maintain high standards with positive ISI inspection outcomes and continuous pastoral reviews.

Name: Keystone Tutors

Name: National Youth Orchestra
Tagline: The National Youth Orchestra - the UK’s leading organisation championing orchestral music as a powerful agent for teenage development.
About: Play your part and support the National Youth Orchestra. Find out how your donation transforms the lives of young people.
Philosophy: The National Youth Orchestra fosters a collaborative and immersive environment, encouraging young musicians to explore their creativity and develop technical skills. Through intensive rehearsals and performances, students gain confidence and a deeper understanding of orchestral music.
Outcomes: Alumni of the National Youth Orchestra often progress to prestigious music conservatoires and universities, including the Royal Academy of Music and Oxbridge. Many have successful careers in leading orchestras and ensembles worldwide, showcasing their exceptional talent and training.
Admissions: Prospective members must audition, demonstrating their musical proficiency and passion for orchestral music. The application process typically opens annually, with auditions held across the UK to ensure accessibility for talented young musicians.
Quote: Unite in harmony to empower young talents, transforming dreams into symphonies with every note we play.

Performance.Student Wellbeing: Apply to join the biggest orchestral music community you’ve ever seen. Accelerate as a musician with world-class tutoring.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: We are all over the nation. Find out where you can join the adventure, and be part of our community and audiences.
Performance.Coaching Credentials: The National Youth Orchestra's coaches include Oxbridge graduates and professional musicians with extensive performance backgrounds in leading UK orchestras. All staff hold DBS checks and relevant teaching qualifications to ensure high-quality instruction.
Performance.Academic Integration: The National Youth Orchestra offers a curriculum that complements A-Level and GCSE studies, focusing on advanced orchestral techniques and music theory. Students engage in immersive workshops that enhance their academic and musical proficiency.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates of the National Youth Orchestra have high acceptance rates to Oxbridge and Russell Group universities, with many securing positions in top-tier orchestras globally. Alumni consistently achieve distinction in music examinations and competitions.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The National Youth Orchestra boasts state-of-the-art rehearsal spaces and a dedicated concert hall in London. These facilities provide students with an inspiring environment to refine their orchestral skills and perform publicly.

Name: The Royal Ballet School
Tagline: As the world’s leading centre for classical ballet training, The Royal Ballet School attracts dancers with exceptional potential from the UK and around the
About: – Dame Ninette de Valois, Founder of The Royal Ballet School and Companies The Royal Ballet School is one of the world’s most celebrated centres for classical ballet training. It is the leading classical ballet school in the UK and an international institution, attracting dancers with exceptional potential from the UK and worldwide.
Philosophy: Since its founding by Dame Ninette de Valois in 1926, the School has contributed immeasurably to our rich dance heritage, producing dancers and choreographers of international renown for generations. Former students include greats like Margot Fonteyn, Anya Linden, Kenneth MacMillan, Lynn Seymour, David Wall, Antoinette Sibley, Anthony Dowell, Marguerite Porter, Stephen Jefferies, Darcey Bussell and Jonathan Cope.
Outcomes: Graduates of The Royal Ballet School often join prestigious companies like The Royal Ballet and Birmingham Royal Ballet. Alumni have achieved international acclaim, contributing significantly to the global ballet scene as principal dancers and choreographers.
Admissions: We welcome applications from all, and no student should be discouraged from applying. Admission to the School is based solely on potential in classical ballet, not personal circumstances, or academic ability.
Quote: Nurturing the grace of tomorrow's ballet legends with passion, precision, and the spirit of artistic excellence.

Performance.Academic Integration: As part of the academic enrichment programme at the Upper School, students may choose to study a range of academic and vocational courses in addition to the degree. One of the options is the Extended Project Qualification, (EPQ).
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Our students come from across the UK and all over the world and are therefore required to live in boarding houses. Younger pupils live within the grounds of White Lodge, Richmond Park and Upper School students live either in Covent Garden or at our new boarding house in Pimlico.
Performance.Coaching Credentials: The Royal Ballet School's faculty includes former principal dancers from prestigious companies and Oxbridge-educated academics, all holding enhanced DBS checks and advanced teaching qualifications to ensure high-quality instruction.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The Royal Ballet School offers a comprehensive wellbeing programme, including a dedicated house system and access to pastoral tutors and professional counselling services to support students' mental and emotional health.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates of The Royal Ballet School often secure positions with leading ballet companies and achieve high acceptance rates at Russell Group universities, contributing significantly to the global ballet community.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: The Royal Ballet School maintains rigorous standards through regular student progress reports, parent consultations, and consistently high ISI inspection outcomes, ensuring continuous quality and accountability in education.

Name: Yehudi Menuhin School
Tagline: A specialist music school in the heart of Surrey, founded in 1963 by celebrated violinist, Yehudi Menuhin
About: School rooms, spaces, boarding facilities and services can be hired for fully catered residential and non-residential small to medium-sized music courses during School holiday periods; these periods include Easter, Summer and Christmas and New Year holiday periods as well as annual half term break weeks in Spring (February and May) and Autumn (October).
Philosophy: The Yehudi Menuhin School nurtures exceptional musical talent through individualized instruction and a holistic curriculum, fostering artistic excellence and personal growth. Students are encouraged to explore their creativity while receiving rigorous academic and musical training.
Outcomes: Graduates often secure places at prestigious conservatoires and universities, including the Royal College of Music and Oxbridge. Alumni have achieved international acclaim, performing with leading orchestras and winning major music competitions worldwide.
Admissions: Prospective students undergo a rigorous audition process, demonstrating exceptional musical ability and potential. Applications are accepted year-round, with auditions typically held in the spring for September entry.
Quote: In harmony, we nurture potential and inspire excellence, fostering a lifelong passion for music and learning.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: The Yehudi Menuhin School's faculty includes Oxbridge graduates and professional musicians with international performance experience. All staff undergo enhanced DBS checks and hold relevant teaching qualifications to ensure high educational standards.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The school offers a robust house system and dedicated pastoral tutors to support student wellbeing. Professional counselling services and wellbeing programmes are available to address emotional and mental health needs.
Performance.Academic Integration: Students pursue a tailored curriculum including GCSEs and A-Levels alongside intensive music training. The school emphasizes a balanced approach, integrating academic excellence with specialist music instruction.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates frequently gain admission to Oxbridge and top Russell Group universities, reflecting strong academic and musical preparation. Alumni have achieved global recognition, performing with prestigious orchestras and winning international competitions.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The Yehudi Menuhin School boasts state-of-the-art concert halls and practice rooms tailored for music education. Additional facilities include a sports hall and theatre, supporting a well-rounded student experience.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Regular progress reports and parent consultations ensure transparent communication about student development. The school consistently receives positive ISI inspection outcomes, reflecting its commitment to maintaining educational quality.

Name: Guildhall School of Music & Drama
Tagline: Guildhall School of Music & Drama is a vibrant, international community of musicians, actors and production artists in the heart of the City of London.
About: Guildhall School of Music & Drama is renowned for its world-class training in music, drama, and production arts, fostering creativity and innovation. Situated in the heart of London, it offers students unparalleled opportunities for artistic and professional development.
Philosophy: The Guildhall School emphasizes a hands-on, experiential learning approach, encouraging students to collaborate across disciplines. Its ethos is rooted in nurturing individual talent while promoting a strong sense of community and artistic excellence.
Outcomes: Graduates of Guildhall School have achieved significant success, with many joining prestigious orchestras, theatre companies, and winning international competitions. Alumni frequently secure positions in leading arts organizations and contribute to the cultural landscape globally.
Admissions: Live auditions in London will take place from November to December 2025. Candidates who apply on time will be notified of their live audition date by no later than 31 October.
Quote: Nurture your unique artistry and transform your passion into a powerful force that resonates worldwide.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Find out more about the courses and programmes on offer at Guildhall, from our undergraduate programmes in Production Arts (with pathways in Costume, Design Realisation, Stage Management or Theatre Technology) and Performance Design, postgraduate degree in Collaborative Performance Making, to short courses, summer schools and training for under-18s.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The Production Arts Department at Guildhall has long been recognised as providing some of the most innovative vocational theatre, arts production and live event training anywhere in the world.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Discover the innovative work of our graduating Production Arts students in their online profiles, from dramas and operas to major public events and collaborative projects.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: Stephen McNeff’s latest opera, A Star Next to the Moon, receives its world premiere at Guildhall School. Based on Juan Rulfo’s iconic novel Pedro Páramo, with a libretto by Aoife Mannix, the piece explores how love and belonging struggle under a tyrannical regime.
Performance.Academic Integration: Guildhall School offers a comprehensive curriculum with specialist subjects in classical music, jazz, and acting, tailored for post-A-Level students. The school emphasizes experiential learning through masterclasses and interdisciplinary projects.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Guildhall graduates frequently join leading orchestras and theatre companies, with alumni often securing roles in West End productions. Many have achieved recognition in international competitions and contribute significantly to the global arts scene.

Name: The Purcell School
Tagline: Welcome to Purcell School!
About: The Purcell School is honoured to have His Majesty King Charles III as our Royal Patron. The former Prince of Wales became Patron of The Purcell School in March 1985 and, following his accession, has confirmed the continued patronage of the School.
Philosophy: From my own experience of a specialist musical education, I know that really dedicated musical pupils will flourish in the specialised environment of The Purcell School. I am very pleased to be able to support the school, and to help as many talented young musicians as possible to enjoy the opportunities available.
Outcomes: Students at The Purcell School are characteristically motivated both musically and academically, and exam results are consistently strong, with excellence in a number of key subjects, including Music, English and Art. Depending on a student’s age and instrumental commitments, approximately a third of curricular time at The Purcell School is spent on musical activities.
Admissions: Entry RequirementsWe are looking for students who can demonstrate their musical skill, desire, commitment and potential. Please see here for further information.
Quote: Use the “Accept” button to consent. Use the “Reject” button to continue without accepting.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: The Purcell School boasts a faculty with Oxbridge degrees and professional performance backgrounds, ensuring high-level musical education. All staff hold DBS checks and relevant teaching qualifications, providing a safe and expert learning environment.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The Purcell School supports student wellbeing through a comprehensive pastoral system, including dedicated house tutors and access to counselling services. Wellbeing programmes focus on mental health, resilience, and personal development.
Performance.Academic Integration: Students pursue a rigorous curriculum offering A-Levels and GCSEs, with a strong emphasis on music, English, and art. The school integrates specialist music training with academic excellence, fostering well-rounded development.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates often secure places at prestigious institutions, including Oxbridge and Russell Group universities, with notable alumni achieving success in various music and arts fields. Exam results consistently reflect high academic standards.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The Purcell School features state-of-the-art facilities, including a concert hall, recording studios, and practice rooms. These resources support both individual practice and ensemble performances, enhancing the musical education experience.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Regular student progress is tracked through detailed reports and parent consultations, ensuring continuous feedback. The school maintains high standards, as evidenced by positive ISI inspection outcomes and regular pastoral reviews.

Name: Royal Academy of Music
Tagline: Nurturing the world's finest musical talent since 1822.
About: The Royal Academy of Music, founded in 1822, is a world-renowned conservatoire dedicated to nurturing exceptional musical talent. It offers a vibrant environment where students engage with leading professionals and explore diverse musical disciplines.
Philosophy: The Academy emphasizes a holistic approach to music education, combining rigorous technical training with creative exploration. It fosters an inclusive community where students are encouraged to innovate and develop their unique artistic voices.
Outcomes: Graduates of the Royal Academy of Music frequently join prestigious ensembles, win international competitions, and secure positions in leading orchestras. The Academy's alumni include celebrated composers, conductors, and performers who have significantly influenced the global music scene.
Admissions: Prospective students must apply through UCAS Conservatoires, showcasing their musical proficiency through auditions and interviews. The application process typically begins in the autumn, with deadlines in early October for courses starting the following year.
Quote: Nurturing the brilliance within, we transform passion into mastery, igniting the world with the power of music.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: The Royal Academy of Music boasts faculty with Oxbridge degrees and extensive professional performance backgrounds, ensuring students receive top-tier instruction. Many staff hold DBS checks and advanced teaching qualifications, enhancing their educational impact.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The Academy provides robust student support through dedicated pastoral tutors and comprehensive counselling services. Wellbeing programmes are integrated into student life, fostering a balanced and supportive environment for all learners.
Performance.Academic Integration: The curriculum at the Royal Academy of Music focuses on specialist musical disciplines, offering advanced training without traditional A-Levels or GCSEs. Students engage in intensive, performance-based learning, tailored to develop professional musicianship.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates frequently secure positions in leading orchestras and ensembles, with many achieving international competition success. Alumni have a strong presence in prestigious institutions, reflecting the Academy's high-caliber training and industry connections.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The Academy features state-of-the-art concert halls and rehearsal spaces, providing students with exceptional venues for practice and performance. Its Marylebone campus is equipped with cutting-edge technology and resources to support musical excellence.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Student progress is meticulously tracked through regular performance assessments and detailed feedback sessions. The Academy maintains high standards, as evidenced by positive ISI inspection outcomes and continuous quality reviews.

Name: Millfield School
Tagline: Millfield is an inspirational school where pupils are encouraged to discover their brilliance.
About: Our next Open Mornings will take place on Saturday 9 May and 3 October 2026. Millfield Pre-Prep's Open Mornings are on Friday 8 May and 2 October 2026.
Philosophy: "Your school is truly incredible and nothing short of awe-inspiring!” "From first contact with Millfield the whole Open Day process, format and content was exceptional.
Outcomes: Whether a Russell-Group university, US university or further education will be your path, preparing for life post-Millfield is made easy with specialist careers advice Leavers to Russell Group and Times Top 30 universities in 2022 We have a busy Summer Term ahead with events including Summer Celebration, Leavers Recital and Summer Production!
Admissions: To ensure a place at Millfield you will need to complete the Registration Form for each student you would like to register. There is a non-refundable fee of £250 per student to register.
Quote: To be able train under the experienced coaches and to follow in the footsteps of the many great athletes who have passed through is incredible.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Millfield's vision for campus development is summarised in Millfield 2035, the school's campus masterplan which identifies future needs and priortises campus developments.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: Millfield's outstanding facilities create two extraordinary campuses which have inspired generations of students to immerse themselves in academic study, culture and sport to express their intellectual, creative and physical potential. Maintaining this calibre of learning environment has been achieved through the school's continued and significant capital investment, alongside generous donations from the wider Millfield community.
Performance.Academic Integration: Millfield School offers a diverse curriculum including A-Levels, BTEC, and GCSEs, with specialist subjects like equine studies and marine science. Innovative teaching methods are employed to cater to a wide range of learning styles.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Millfield School boasts a strong record of graduates attending Russell Group universities, with notable alumni excelling in various fields. A significant percentage of students achieve top grades in A-Levels and GCSEs annually.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: The campus features state-of-the-art facilities including an Olympic-sized swimming pool, a theatre, and extensive sports grounds. Millfield's music department is equipped with a concert hall and recording studios.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Student progress is closely monitored through regular reports and bi-annual parent consultations. The school consistently receives excellent ratings in ISI inspections, reflecting its commitment to maintaining high educational standards.

Name: Wellington College
Tagline: We believe that every child has immense potential that, in the right environment, can be uncovered, nurtured and realised.
About: Wellington College is one of the UK’s — and indeed the world’s — great co-educational boarding and day schools. The College is set in 400 acres of beautiful parkland and is home to a blend of breathtaking original architecture and modern state-of-the-art facilities to provide the best possible educational environment for our pupils.
Philosophy: True to the classical roots of the word ‘education’, the College aims to provide an ambitious and aspirational environment in which the potential of each pupil is ‘brought out’ and developed across all aspects of themselves as unique individuals. At Wellington the pervading culture of ambition and aspiration creates an atmosphere which not only encourages every pupil, teacher, and parent to believe anything is possible, but also recognises that each Wellingtonian has immense potential which must be nurtured and realised.
Outcomes: “If I had to put my finger on the one aspect of Wellington College that excites me most, it is the all-pervading culture of ambition and aspiration, an atmosphere which encourages every pupil, teacher and parent to believe that anything is possible.” pupils and parents in our global professional and social network A thriving school of over 1,100 students, with 18 Houses that are small enough for each child to feel at home – a place where they are known, supported, and part of a close-knit community.
Admissions: "Discovering whether Wellington is the right fit for your child lies at the heart of our admissions process. We want to offer places to children who will be really happy here, and who have the desire and determination to make the most of all the opportunities on offer to them." We are delighted that a restructuring of our boarding houses has enabled us to create a small number of places for entry at 14+.
Quote: Wellington gives everyone the platform to explore and develop their own passions.

Performance.Coaching Credentials: Wellington College boasts a distinguished faculty with many educators holding Oxbridge degrees and extensive professional backgrounds. All staff are DBS-checked and possess relevant teaching qualifications, ensuring high-quality education and mentorship.
Performance.Student Wellbeing: The College's robust house system and dedicated pastoral tutors provide comprehensive support for student wellbeing. Additionally, counselling services and wellbeing programmes are integral to nurturing students' mental and emotional health.
Performance.Academic Integration: Wellington College offers a broad curriculum including A-Levels and GCSEs, with a strong emphasis on interdisciplinary learning. Specialist subjects such as STEM and the arts are taught using innovative methods to enhance engagement.
Performance.Competitive Pathway: Graduates from Wellington College frequently secure places at Oxbridge and Russell Group universities, reflecting the school's strong academic performance. The College's alumni network includes leaders in various professional fields, showcasing diverse career pathways.
Performance.Facilities & Resources: Set in 400 acres, Wellington College features state-of-the-art facilities including a concert hall, sports complex, and modern science labs. The campus also houses a theatre and swimming pool, supporting diverse extracurricular activities.
Performance.Ongoing Accountability: Student progress at Wellington College is closely monitored through regular reports and parent consultations. The College consistently receives excellent ISI inspection outcomes, reflecting its commitment to maintaining high educational standards.
